title: SpaceX History Update — Mar 29, 2026
summary: SpaceX launched its 38th mission of 2026 on March 26 with Starlink 17-17 from Vandenberg, booster B1081's 23rd flight, achieving its 591st total booster recovery (186th on OCISLY). The week's biggest corporate news came from Gwynne Shotwell's TIME magazine cover story (March 26), which revealed SpaceX filed an FCC application to license up to 1 million AI-powered orbital data center satellites, and that SpaceX is scheduling investor briefings for April ahead of an IPO targeting up to $75 billion — with the combined SpaceX+xAI entity valued at $1.25–1.75 trillion. The Transporter-16 rideshare mission (119 payloads) was scrubbed twice on March 23 and 25 due to winds and a boat in the keep-out zone. Booster B1067's record-setting 34th flight on Starlink 10-44 was delayed multiple times, slipping to late March. KPIs, claims, and political profiles were updated to reflect the xAI merger, IPO developments, and Shotwell's new corporate role.

title: SpaceX History Update — Mar 26, 2026
summary: SpaceX crossed a landmark milestone on March 16–17, 2026, when a Falcon 9 Starlink Group 17-24 mission from Vandenberg Space Force Base pushed the active Starlink constellation past 10,000 satellites simultaneously in orbit — less than seven years after the first Starlink launch and now representing roughly two-thirds of all active satellites worldwide. SpaceX continued its extraordinary 2026 launch cadence with Falcon 9 missions on March 19 (29 satellites from Cape Canaveral), March 20 (25 satellites from Vandenberg, booster B1100's 4th flight), and March 22 (Starlink 10-62, booster B1078's record 27th flight from Cape Canaveral). Meanwhile, Starship Flight 12 — the debut of the upgraded V3 configuration with Raptor 3 engines — was pushed from a March target to approximately April 2026 following a booster anomaly during pressure testing and subsequent FAA licensing for the April 5–October 5, 2026 window.
